title: shorea megistophylla
intro: Shorea megistophylla, also known as Belangkas, is a species of tree in the family Dipterocarpaceae, native to the tropical forests of Borneo. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high content of saponins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Shorea megistophylla include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat wounds, making it a valuable resource for local communities. Herbal preparations of Shorea megistophylla can be made by infusing the bark or leaves in hot water, or by extracting the saponins and flavonoids to create a medicinal extract.
